Section 1: Project Identification
Project EA: 04-1A2B3C / Project Number: 0421000123 / District: 04
Subject: I-880 Northbound Operational Improvements Phase II
Section 2: Project Description
The proposed project widens approximately 3.2 miles of I-880 Northbound between Mowry Avenue and Industrial Parkway in the city of Fremont. Work includes adding a fourth mainline lane in each direction, replacing two undersized cross-culverts under the BNSF rail corridor, and reconstructing two interchange ramps with longer acceleration / deceleration lanes.
Section 3: Project Status
Currently at 30% design. PA&ED was approved in March 2025. The project is fully funded through SB-1 and federal STBG sources for design; construction capital allocation pending CTC vote.
Section 4: Financial Summary
The engineer's estimate for construction capital is $48.7 million. Right-of-way costs are estimated at an additional $9.2 million across 14 partial takings.
Section 5: Schedule
The district intends to advertise within 24 months of receiving the construction allocation. Schedule compression beyond that window would require a delivery method that allows early packages.
Section 6: Risk and Complexity
The project involves coordination with BNSF railroad for two track-closure windows totaling 96 hours, PG&E gas line relocation across two parcels, and EBMUD water main relocation. Multiple third-party utility relocations are anticipated.
Section 7: Right of Way
Fourteen partial takings are required, two of which involve commercial properties with established businesses. Loss-of-business-goodwill claims are anticipated on at least one parcel.
Section 8: Environmental Permitting
The project requires Section 404 permits and a Caltrans Streambed Alteration Agreement. Environmental clearance is complete; only ministerial permits remain.
Section 9: Innovation Opportunities
Performance specifications are appropriate for the new pavement section and the bridge deck overlay. The cross-culvert replacements have several feasible alternative configurations.
Section 10: Quality Considerations
The district seeks long-term pavement performance and is open to higher-quality cross-culvert designs (precast vs cast-in-place) that could reduce future maintenance.
Section 11: Procurement and Staffing
The district has a four-person resident-engineer team and an experienced construction-management group. Headquarters procurement support is fully available.
Section 12: Funding Status
Design funding is committed. Construction funding is identified but not yet allocated; CTC vote anticipated within 12 months.
Project Risk Assessment
[This section and all subsequent sections (13-15) should be excluded from the AI evaluation.]
